hero.headline: Why not go solar?
hero.subhead: Premium solar, air-con and electrical across Brisbane and surrounds. Same kit we wire into 100kW commercial jobs. Backed by our 10 Year Installation Warranty.
hero.canvas_spec: Single Canvas 2D layer, full-bleed behind hero copy, driven by ONE scroll-progress variable p (0..1) mapped from window.scrollY / heroHeight. At p=0 render ~90 cyan voltage-arc segments (zig-zag bezier paths, stroke #00D9FF at 0.35 alpha, lineWidth 1.5, additive blend) that lerp gently toward mouseY with proximityFactor; at p=1 render the same particle pool as warm sun rays radiating from a fixed sun-anchor at (0.7vw, 0.3vh), stroke #FFB800 at 0.45 alpha, no cursor reactivity. Each particle's hue/colour interpolates HSL(190,100%,50%)->HSL(40,100%,50%) by p; arc-jaggedness interpolates to ray-straightness by p. Backdrop is hero photo (03-DYwKM2hGsQd.webp — SolaX 50kWh whole-home battery + LONGi panels) at 0.35 opacity over #0A0B0F. IntersectionObserver pauses rAF off-screen; prefers-reduced-motion renders one static composite (arcs+rays both at 0.5 alpha, no animation); on touch devices (matchMedia('(hover: hover)') false) cursor-bias is disabled but scroll morph stays; canvas sized to devicePixelRatio with willReadFrequently:false; resize debounced 250ms.

palette.primary_role: Electrical lineage — voltage arcs, link underlines, focus rings, primary CTA border-glow, count-up numerals, suburb-check success tick
palette.secondary_role: Solar lineage — sun rays, savings calculator fill, warranty badge, kW figures in stat rows

scale.load_strategy: Self-host both via @font-face with font-display:swap. Preload Manrope 700 and Inter 400 (subset latin). Manrope weights 500/600/700/800; Inter 400/500/600.
scale.display_settings: Manrope display defaults: font-weight 700, letter-spacing -0.025em, line-height 0.98, text-wrap balance, hyphens none.
scale.body_settings: Inter body defaults: font-weight 400, letter-spacing -0.005em, line-height 1.55, text-wrap pretty.
scale.--text-base: 1rem / 16px · lh 1.55 (body; MIN on inputs to stop iOS zoom)

sections.title: Why Shocked — the standard
sections.layout: section, surface bg, 140px padding. Two-col 7/5 desktop. Left: eyebrow + H2 + 4 body paragraphs in long-form. Right: sticky-position stat panel with 4 count-up tiles (2x2 grid) — 100 kW · largest install / 96 kWh · biggest battery / 10 yr · installation warranty / 5+ · tier-1 brands. Below: photo of one real install full-bleed across the section.

copy.h2: Same kit on your roof as on a commercial site.
body_paragraphs: The team started Shocked Solar & Electrical to do solar properly. Premium kit, clean installs, honest pricing. No commodity tier.

We have tackled a 100kW remote North Queensland job on Jinko, Schletter and Sigenergy with 96kWh of battery storage. We have wired up a 21kW commercial site in Mount Gravatt on TCL 440W panels and a GoodWe 3-phase inverter. We have run a 13.3kW LONGi Hi-MO X10 system into a 50kWh SolaX battery for whole-home backup.

We have rethought a system on site to a landscape config when wind ratings changed the game. We have relocated split systems to clear room for a monster fly-over patio, then roughed in and fit off the kitchenette behind it.
Big jobs, small jobs, residential or commercial. Every install backed by our 10 Year Installation Warranty.

component_techniques.01_hero_canvas_morph: Single Canvas 2D, devicePixelRatio-aware sizing, single rAF loop. Particle pool of ~90 objects {baseX, baseY, x, y, vx, vy, jag, hue}. p = clamp(scrollY/heroH, 0, 1). For each particle: targetX biased by ±jag*(1-p) (arcs are wavy, rays straight); colour = lerpHSL(190->40, by p); cursor-bias only when p<0.5 AND matchMedia('(hover:hover)'). ctx.globalCompositeOperation='lighter' for additive glow. Wrap entire RAF in IntersectionObserver pause/resume. prefers-reduced-motion bypasses RAF — renders one static composite frame. Resize debounced 250ms. Code budget ~120 lines.
component_techniques.02_suburb_check_combobox: Native input + JS-enhanced listbox. ARIA combobox pattern (role=combobox, aria-autocomplete=list, aria-expanded, aria-controls, aria-activedescendant). Local SUBURBS array (see suburbs[]). Filter: 150ms debounce, startsWith pass then contains pass, top 8. Keyboard: Up/Down/Home/End/Esc/Enter. autocomplete='address-level2', inputmode='text', autocapitalize='words', font-size 16px+. Listbox max-height 40vh, 5 results on mobile. On submit/select: setTimeout 250ms then render success card (green tick) or soft-no (amber dot, never blocks). Write suburb to sessionStorage('ssec_suburb'). aria-live=polite on result panel.
component_techniques.03_magnetic_cta: Per-button gsap.quickTo on x,y (duration 0.6, ease power3). Single document pointermove listener uses e.target.closest('.magnetic'). distance = Math.hypot(dx, dy); if distance<120 then setX(dx*0.4), setY(dy*0.4); else setX(0), setY(0). Inner label span translates at 0.7x parent for split-magnet feel. Disabled via @media(hover:none) and prefers-reduced-motion. Focus ring positioned on un-translated element. will-change:transform on hover-in only.
component_techniques.04_count_up_stat: Single shared IntersectionObserver, threshold 0.4 desktop / 0.3 mobile, fires once per element (data-counted='1' guard). rAF loop interpolating 0 to data-target with easeOutExpo over 1800ms (1200ms mobile). Math.round each frame, textContent only. Final value written from data-target post-animation to avoid drift. Format with Intl.NumberFormat('en-AU'). Set aria-label on wrapper combining figure + context. prefers-reduced-motion: jump to final value, no animation.
component_techniques.05_service_card_spotlight: Per-card pointermove sets --card-mx, --card-my as % of card bounds (namespaced to avoid clobbering global spotlight). ::before pseudo background:radial-gradient(circle 320px at var(--card-mx) var(--card-my), rgba(0,217,255,0.10), transparent 60%). Hover: translateY(-6px) + box-shadow var(--glow-cyan) + border-color var(--lines-strong). Disabled on @media(hover:none) then :active scale(0.98). Card is single <a> wrapping all content.
component_techniques.06_savings_calculator: Native <input type=range> min=3 max=20 step=0.5 value=6.6. CSS gradient fill via --fillPct var. Brisbane constants: PEAK_SUN_HRS=5.0, TARIFF_KWH=0.30, FEED_IN=0.06, SELF_CONSUMP={no_batt:0.4, 13.5:0.65, 27:0.85}. annualSavings = kW * PEAK_SUN_HRS * 365 * (SELF * TARIFF + (1-SELF) * FEED_IN). 25-year = annual * 25 * 0.92 (panel degradation factor). Payback = installCost / annualSavings where installCost = 1200 * kW + (battery=='13.5'?12500:battery=='27'?22000:0) - stcRebate. rAF-eased count-up (lerp 0.18), Intl.NumberFormat('en-AU', {style:'currency', currency:'AUD', maximumFractionDigits:0}). Disclaimer visible inline.
component_techniques.07_recent_work_lightbox: Pure vanilla. Overlay div (position:fixed, bg rgba(10,11,15,0.94), backdrop-filter blur 8px). On tile click open with current photo. Esc closes, click-out closes, left/right arrow keys navigate, focus-trap with tabindex. Loads full-res from .jpg (640px source already optimised). Lazy-mounts on first open. ScrollTrigger.refresh() on close. aria-modal=true, aria-label='Project photo viewer'.
component_techniques.08_voltage_divider_svg: Hand-authored zig-zag SVG path, total length via getTotalLength(). stroke-dasharray + stroke-dashoffset both = length initially. On IntersectionObserver enter (threshold 0.3), transition stroke-dashoffset 1400ms ease-out to 0. Second path copy with stroke #00D9FF + filter:drop-shadow(0 0 6px currentColor), 200ms delay, glow trail. aria-hidden='true', focusable='false'. Skip animation via prefers-reduced-motion (set offset to 0 immediately). One animated divider per visible viewport max (perf budget on drop-shadow filter).

animation_choreography: "INIT (all on DOMContentLoaded + fonts.ready): 1) Lenis init lerp 0.09, smoothWheel true, smoothTouch false (iOS swipe-back conflict). 2) gsap.ticker.add(time => lenis.raf(time*1000)); gsap.ticker.lagSmoothing(0). 3) lenis.on('scroll', ScrollTrigger.update). 4) ScrollTrigger.refresh() after fonts.ready and on window 'load'. 5) Wrap each headline in word/inner spans BEFORE animation. PAGE ENTRY (0 to 1200ms): Hero canvas starts rAF immediately. H1 word-stagger reveal (gsap.from .inner, y:'100%' opacity:0, stagger:0.04, expo.out, duration:0.9). Subhead fade-in delay:0.4. Suburb-check fade-up delay:0.7. Trust-pill scale-in delay:0.9. Nav fade-in opacity 0->1 delay:0.2. SCROLL (heroH 0->1): Canvas voltage to sun morph driven by p=scrollY/heroH. Body bg gradient interpolates --bg-start, --bg-end via single ScrollTrigger scrub:1 timeline. Past 70vh: nav .scrolled class adds backdrop blur + condensed suburb-check slides in. Past hero: mobile call bar reveals via IntersectionObserver on hero sentinel. SECTION ENTRIES (start: top 80%): Each section eyebrow + H2 word-stagger. Card grids stagger 0.06-0.12 y:32->0 expo.out. Count-up stats fire on threshold 0.4. Photos lazy-load and fade-in opacity 0->1 200ms. Voltage SVG dividers draw-in 1400ms ease-out on enter. CONTINUOUS: Magnetic CTAs (only above the fold + closing banner). Cursor spotlight on document.body for desktop (CSS-only radial-gradient at --mx --my, mix-blend-mode:screen, opacity 0.15, fixed overlay pointer-events:none). Service card per-card spotlights. Marquee trust strip 50s linear infinite. Leaflet marker pulse 1800ms infinite. SCROLL-SCRUBBED: Why-Shocked photo parallax (translateY scrub:0.6, -40px to 40px). CTA banner background subtle parallax (scrub:0.8). REDUCED MOTION: matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') cancels ALL of: canvas rAF (one static composite), word-stagger reveals (snap to final via gsap.set), count-ups (snap), marquee (stop), magnetic (off), parallax (off), Leaflet marker pulse (off). Word reveals still render text."

faq.q: Do I still get the federal solar rebate?
faq.a: Yes. The federal small-scale rebate (STCs) is currently available on new systems and we handle the paperwork for you. You see the discount up front in the quote, not as a refund later. We will walk you through the exact rebate your system is eligible for before you sign anything.

Yes. The federal Cheaper Home Batteries Program is currently available on new battery installs alongside solar. The exact dollar value depends on the battery size and which tier the scheme is in at the time of install, so we calculate it fresh at quote time and show it on the line item.

faq.q: Do I really need a battery, or just panels?
faq.a: Depends on your bills, your usage and whether you want the lights on when the grid goes down. We have done full whole-home backup with 50kWh SolaX and we have done panels-only where it made more sense. Honest advice on the quote, not a sales pitch.

faq.q: What brands do you actually install?
faq.a: SolaX, GoodWe, Jinko, LONGi, TCL and Sigenergy for solar and batteries. Schletter for racking. We pick the brand to suit the job, not the margin. Every panel and inverter we put up is a Tier-1 bankable brand with full Australian warranty support.

faq.q: How do I know what size air-con I need?
faq.a: Room size, ceiling height, insulation, window orientation and how many people use the space. The team measures on site and sizes the head properly. Undersized units run flat out and die early. Oversized ones short-cycle. We get it right the first time.

faq.q: Can you upgrade my switchboard at the same time as solar?
faq.a: Often yes. On a lot of older homes you have to, because the existing board cannot safely handle a new inverter or battery. If that is the case we will flag it up front in the quote and arrange it. One crew, one visit, one cost. No surprises mid-install.

faq.q: How long does an install take?
faq.a: A standard residential solar system is usually a one-day install once your STC paperwork, your retailer paperwork and our material order are sorted. Bigger jobs and battery installs can run two to three days. We give you a real timeline in the quote, not a guess.

faq.q: What does the 10 Year Installation Warranty cover?
faq.a: Everything the team puts up. Mounting and racking integrity, cable, conduit and isolator workmanship, roof penetration waterproofing, and all electrical connections. Manufacturer warranties on panels, inverters and batteries are separate and we walk you through those at handover.

faq.q: What areas do you actually cover?
faq.a: Brisbane south, Logan, Ipswich east and Scenic Rim for everyday solar, air-con and electrical work. For larger commercial and off-grid jobs we travel further, including remote North Queensland. If you sit outside the standard run, ring us and we will talk it through.

faq.q: How quickly can you get out for a quote?
faq.a: Call 0490 482 632 or send the form and we will lock in a time that suits. We will always give you a real answer on timing rather than a vague promise.

au_facts.stc_rebate: Federal Small-scale Technology Certificate (STC) rebate is currently available on new solar installs and applied as an up-front discount at point of sale. Approximate per-kW value mid-2026 in Zone 3 (Brisbane): ~$259-$378/kW depending on daily STC spot price (~$37-$39) and the 7-year deeming period. Indicative figures: a 6.6kW system attracts roughly $1,710-$2,500; a 13.2kW system roughly $3,420-$5,000. STC eligibility caps at 100kW system size. The exact dollar value must be calculated at quote time using the REC Registry SGU/STC calculator. Never lock dollar amounts into hero copy without an 'approx' qualifier.
au_facts.battery_rebate: Federal Cheaper Home Batteries Program launched 1 July 2025 and is the primary battery incentive nationally. Pre-1-May-2026: ~$302/kWh discount (STC factor 8.4), capped ~$16,500 up to 50kWh usable. From 1 May 2026 a tiered structure applies (0-14kWh @ 100% STC factor, 14-28kWh @ 60%, 28-50kWh @ 15%) with the STC factor stepping down every six months. There is no QLD state solar rebate and no QLD state battery rebate in 2026. The QLD Battery Booster closed when the federal program launched. On-site copy must say 'federal rebate' and pull current tier at quote time.
au_facts.warranty_norms: Australian solar warranty industry standards split four ways: (1) Panel PRODUCT warranty: Tier-1 brands 12-25 years, premium 25-30. (2) Panel PERFORMANCE warranty: minimum 25 years mandated AU, 90% at year 10 / 80% at year 25. (3) Inverter warranty: 5-10 years string, 10-12 premium (SMA, Fronius, Sungrow, GoodWe), 10-25 microinverters/hybrids. (4) Workmanship/Installation warranty: 5 years industry standard, 5-10 years reputable, 10-year is premium positioning. A '10-year installation warranty' typically covers mounting and racking integrity, cable/conduit/isolator workmanship, roof penetration waterproofing, electrical connections, and rectification of installer-caused defects. It does NOT cover manufacturer faults on panels/inverters/batteries (those go via product warranty). Shocked's 10 Year Installation Warranty is at the premium end of the AU market and is a defensible differentiator.
au_facts.qld_avg_bill: Approximately $1,500 to $1,900/year for a typical 2-4 person Energex SEQ household on the FY2026 Default Market Offer. Average usage rate ~27-30c/kWh.
au_facts.typical_6_6kw_savings: A 6.6kW system in Brisbane generates ~27.7 kWh/day, ~10,100 kWh/year. Annual savings typically $1,400 to $2,400 depending on self-consumption vs export mix. Mid-range estimate $1,800 to $2,200/year. Payback period 3-5 years on current tariffs and STC rebate.
au_facts.ac_sizing_guide: Rule of thumb: ~125-150W per m2 for standard 2.4m ceiling, insulated room. Brisbane sub-tropical climate adds load. Round UP, not down. Indicative kW per room size: 10m2 = 2.0-2.5kW (bedroom/office); 20m2 = 2.5-3.5kW; 30m2 = 3.5-5.0kW; 50m2 = 5.0-7.2kW (open-plan, 6.0kW typical); 80m2 = 8.0-10.0kW (often ducted or multi-split at this size). Window orientation (west-facing glass) and ceiling height materially affect required capacity. Always subject to site assessment.
au_facts.standards_compliance: All work to AS/NZS 3000:2018 (Wiring Rules), AS/NZS 5033:2021 (PV array installation), AS/NZS 4777.1 and 4777.2:2020 (grid-connection inverters). Energex/SEQ DNSP export limits apply (single-phase typically 5kW inverter, three-phase up to 15kW; dynamic connections now default).
tier1_brands_shocked_installs: Sigenergy (inverter + 96kWh battery proven on 100kW NQ install)
au_facts.service_area_basis: Brisbane south + Logan + Ipswich east + Scenic Rim within ~30km radius of Boronia Heights 4124 (Logan City). Larger commercial and off-grid work travelled further including remote North Queensland (case study basis, not a standard service area).
flags_for_build: Verify Shocked's SAA/CEC installer accreditation number BEFORE displaying any CEC/SAA badge or claiming accreditation in copy.
Verify Master Electricians Australia membership BEFORE displaying MEA badge or 'Master Electrician' claim.
Confirm '10 Year Installation Warranty' is issued in writing as a customer document before publishing on site.
Verify ABN 27 684 898 762 on abr.business.gov.au before publishing in footer/schema.
Verify LONGi Hi-MO X10 SKU is the actual install kit (caption confirms; keep as-is unless contradicted at sign-off).
STC rebate $/kW figures fluctuate with STC spot price. Use 'approx' framing or pull live at quote time. Never hard-code a single dollar value into hero copy.
Federal Cheaper Home Batteries tier steps down every 6 months from May 2026. Disclaimer line must be present anywhere a battery rebate dollar value appears.

inspiration_patterns.pattern: Single-canvas morph driven by ONE scroll progress variable (NOT crossfade of two scenes)
inspiration_patterns.implementation: Inspired by Younergy + Dit is Watt. Canvas 2D, full-bleed hero, ~90 particles in a shared pool. p = clamp(scrollY / heroHeight, 0, 1). Same particles morph: at p=0 they render as cyan voltage-arc segments (jagged zig-zag bezier, additive blend); at p=1 they render as warm amber sun rays (straight lines from sun anchor, additive blend). Particle hue interpolates HSL(190,100%,50%) to HSL(40,100%,50%) by p. Cursor-bias active only when p<0.5. IntersectionObserver pauses RAF off-screen. prefers-reduced-motion bypasses RAF entirely. One static composite frame with both states at 0.5 alpha.
inspiration_patterns.where: Hero section only (top 100svh). Backdrop is real install photo (assets/gallery/03-DYwKM2hGsQd.webp) at 0.35 opacity so the canvas reads as 'energy flowing across our real work', not as a tech demo.

inspiration_patterns.implementation: Inspired by Octopus + Sunrun, distilled to single-step. Native input + ARIA combobox + JS-enhanced listbox. Local SUBURBS array (96 entries, ~5KB). 150ms debounced filter, top 8 results. On submit: 250ms simulated query then success card (cyan tick + 'Yep, we cover [Suburb]. Lock in a free quote and we will lock in a time.' + button that scrolls to quote form) OR soft-no (amber dot + '[Suburb] is outside our standard run, but we travel for the bigger jobs. Give us a ring.' + tel button). Selected suburb saved to sessionStorage('ssec_suburb'), prefilled into quote form. Never blocks. aria-live=polite on result panel. Field font-size 16px+, tap target 48px+, autocomplete='address-level2'.
inspiration_patterns.where: Above-the-fold inside hero, replacing the typical 'Get a Quote' button. Re-shows in condensed form (input + button, 44px tall) inside the sticky nav past 70vh scroll. This is THE primary conversion entry point.

inspiration_patterns.pattern: Editorial restraint with three beats of certainty + literal photographic proof grid
inspiration_patterns.implementation: Inspired by Tesla Energy + Sunrun. Hero subhead carries the three beats inline ('Solar. Power. Comfort.' as a supporting strip). Mid-page Recent Work grid is the literal payoff. 1 hero tile + 5 supporting tiles in asymmetric CSS grid, every tile a REAL install with kW chip + brand spec list overlaid. No stock photography anywhere on the page. This positions Shocked against the 7 audited competitors who all hide spec sheets behind quote forms. Verifiable hardware honesty becomes the differentiator. Builds direct on the audit finding: 'NOBODY shows specific kW system specs, real brand partners or installed-job data on the homepage hero.'
inspiration_patterns.where: Three beats: hero subhead strip + footer brand tag. Photographic proof: recent-work grid section + why-shocked full-bleed image + closing CTA banner background. Every photo path locked from /assets/gallery/. No AI imagery, no stock, no fakes.
